Charges: Unlimited

Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

QuoteReply
# Jun 10 2003 at 3:50 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591775009710
8 posts
Purchased for 300pp in the Bazaar during the weekend. A lighter tint of gold/bronze than the Totemic Shaman armor, but matches quite nicely.

Lvl 19 Shaman - Solusek Ro

price

# Jun 11 2002 at 11:09 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592759319543
2 posts
Bought mine on Tunare server for 300 pp I would like to get the whole set, if only I can get deals that sweet :)
▲ Page top
Deal
QuoteReply
# Apr 10 2002 at 1:35 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249975094
18 posts
I picked this up and a JB helm for a total of 1750pp on Bristlebane. I am only 31 at the time, but look forwar to picking up as much JB as I can so at 45 I will be a casting ****...hehe!
▲ Page top
Self or not?
QuoteReply
# Feb 15 2002 at 5:21 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591775142073
5 posts
I've been wondering about all the Jaundice armor. Is the buff self only or are you also able to target other players and buff them too?
▲ Page top
RE: you can target others
QuoteReply
# Apr 10 2002 at 3:45 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1595780190282
View User Forum
26 posts
Yes you can target others.
Clickable effect at 45.
Casting time is double of what the spell is.
Yes they are nice items for buffing. I want the whole set for buffing and attack.

Arms= STR
Feet= AGI
Hands= DEX
Legs= STA +8dex/str/wis and 75mana
Helm= CHA
Chest= Dmg Shield +10wis/agi 20 hp and 80mana
Bracer= 256DD

I still need the arms, bracer, chest, and legs. Im not interested in getting the helm.

This is not worth the 2k price tag that is currently shown, Dont pay more then 750pp for this. Casting group strength is easier and many of the tanks I run across have some kind of similar self buff. Good luck gathering this set of armor it will be tough but worth the effort. Yea there is better out there for you to wear, but I carry these around for mana less buffing.

People are really supprised when you fully buff 12 people and still have mana for others. Experience from being the only shaman on a raid and buffing everyone.


Edited, Wed Apr 10 18:53:28 2002
▲ Page top
for sale
QuoteReply
# Jan 27 2002 at 6:47 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592252813383
159 posts
i have these for sale on tribunal or will trade for halfling druid stuff
▲ Page top
damn...
QuoteReply
# Jan 27 2002 at 3:02 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591773687423
125 posts
why is it that Cobalt is way more expensive then all the other class specific armors? Sorta odd lookin at the LARGE price difference to the rest.
▲ Page top
RE: damn...
QuoteReply
# Feb 09 2002 at 9:37 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592758369942
Scholar
71 posts
cause a lot more people have Warrior twinks =D
▲ Page top
RE: damn...
QuoteReply
# Apr 05 2002 at 4:23 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361516680
Scholar
19 posts
Actually, its because the AC on Cobalt is higher than a lot of other warrior armours and AC is most important to high level tanks. Also, some of the effects are quite useful to a warrior.
▲ Page top
RE: damn...
QuoteReply
# Aug 17 2004 at 8:12 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592257248882
Scholar
155 posts
There is also the fact that shammy can cast the buffs on thier Kunark armor.. warriors can't. It is far more valuable to a warrior to be able to self buff and self heal than it is a shaman, since they already HAVE the spells.. now the bracer is another story.. mana free nuke is always good...

▲ Page top
My lucky day(s)
QuoteReply
# Jan 21 2002 at 10:40 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592251064527
110 posts
I was checking the merchants in Kelethin last week and I found one of these babies at a merchant for only 362p and some gold. WOW! So, I looked at it, and decided not to get it. I logged off and went to sleep. Then, the next day I talked to my friend who has a Shaman, and he said I was stupid not to buy them. Anyway, fast forward to late that night and guess what, they are STILL at the merchant! I quickly bought them and sold them to my friend for 365p.

I wonder a) Why anyone sold this to a merchant and b) why anyone didn't buy them in OVER 24 hours time!?!?!? There's a moral to this: Check those merchants!
▲ Page top
RE: My lucky day(s)
QuoteReply
# Mar 07 2002 at 4:21 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592757172605
View User Forum
21 posts
I do sell that kind of items occationally to merchants if
a. It is lore and
b. It is common and
c. If the merchant offers a fair price.

But definetely spent some time with merchants, cause I heard many true stories like Megladar the Mighty had.
▲ Page top
I just KEEP getting LUCKY
QuoteReply
# Oct 25 2001 at 3:25 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1593361699639
View User Forum
182 posts
As a 9th level Shaman I was with a group of other Shamen out on the shelf by Bandl McMarrin, whacking what beasties we could. Along came a 50 something shaman in polar bear form. Marveling at such a large group of young shamen he offered to give us a quiz in exchange for "some useful items." The question he asked me was, "At what level can a Shaman summon a pet, and what should it be? I told him that in our 29th season we could befriend an animal, and that for a Barbarian Shaman that pet "should" naturally be a...Polar Bear! He smiled and GAVE me these vambraces! He also reminded me that the effect does not proc until 45 (a LONG time for me!), but the armor class is excellent from now until then. I never beg...but I guess being polite and as nice as possible does have good side effects.

Macavitie, 16th level Barbarian Shaman on Veeshan.

"But when you reach the scene of the crime, Macavitie's Not there!"
▲ Page top
RE: I just KEEP getting LUCKY
QuoteReply
# Jan 19 2002 at 2:19 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591770319679
4 posts
This is a very nice story, and the elder shaman who gave you this piece of armor sure was generous--however you answered the question incorrectly (although your answer was MUCH more clever than the actual answer I admit). As you might know, Befriend Animal is not a SUMMON spell, it is a charm. His question was "at what level can a shaman SUMMON a pet," and the answer of course is at level 34 when we get Companion Spirit, and yes, it SHOULD be a Polar Bear but for some reason VI gave us a Druid-type pet (Wolf). Cheers.
▲ Page top
soo lucky
QuoteReply
# Aug 12 2001 at 1:49 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591762513300
3 posts
i got these for 800, and the gauntlets for 700. VERY lucky

lopez
41 shaman
morell-thule

RE: soo lucky
QuoteReply
# Nov 10 2002 at 6:05 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593362161379
43 posts
hmm, how lucky are you? i've been seeing these and the helms go for 100-300 consistantly. Of course i've seen several of these at once, but some of the other pieces (namely the BP) i have never seen available. If you ask me, 700pp is too much.
▲ Page top
RE: soo lucky
QuoteReply
# Feb 21 2003 at 7:55 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591776410526
Sage
280 posts
Only a tard would sell JB gear for 100pp as even vendors will pay like 300-400pp per piece of average JB gear with any decent CHA. Get your self a couple of those +25 CHA steins and put up a CHA buff next time you go see a vendor you will get much better prices. Even this Ogre shammy with only 42 starting CHA can get to 146 CHA which is enough to get the best price on an item usually.

58 Ogre Luminary on Luclin server--Chaotic Winds--Venerable Aggrok
